query:
INSERT INTO quiz_questions (lessonId, question, questionType, options, correctAnswer, questionOrder) VALUES
-- GED-SOCIAL Lesson 1: US History Foundations (600001)
(600001, 'The Declaration of Independence was signed in:', 'multiple_choice', '["1775","1776","1777","1787"]', '1776', 1),
(600001, 'The Constitution replaced the:', 'multiple_choice', '["Declaration of Independence","Articles of Confederation","Bill of Rights","Magna Carta"]', 'Articles of Confederation', 2),
(600001, 'The Bill of Rights consists of:', 'multiple_choice', '["The first 5 amendments","The first 10 amendments","The first 15 amendments","The first 20 amendments"]', 'The first 10 amendments', 3),
(600001, 'The three branches of government are:', 'multiple_choice', '["Federal, State, Local","Executive, Legislative, Judicial","President, Congress, Courts","Senate, House, Cabinet"]', 'Executive, Legislative, Judicial', 4),
(600001, 'The Civil War was primarily fought over:', 'multiple_choice', '["Taxation","Slavery and states rights","Foreign policy","Trade agreements"]', 'Slavery and states rights', 5),
(600001, 'The Emancipation Proclamation freed:', 'multiple_choice', '["All slaves","Slaves in Confederate states","Northern slaves","No one"]', 'Slaves in Confederate states', 6),
(600001, 'The Industrial Revolution brought:', 'multiple_choice', '["Agricultural decline","Factory-based manufacturing","Less urbanization","Fewer inventions"]', 'Factory-based manufacturing', 7),
(600001, 'World War I began in:', 'multiple_choice', '["1912","1914","1916","1918"]', '1914', 8),
(600001, 'The Great Depression started with:', 'multiple_choice', '["World War I","Stock market crash of 1929","World War II","The New Deal"]', 'Stock market crash of 1929', 9),
(600001, 'The New Deal was created by President:', 'multiple_choice', '["Herbert Hoover","Franklin D. Roosevelt","Harry Truman","Dwight Eisenhower"]', 'Franklin D. Roosevelt', 10),
(600001, 'Explain the significance of the Constitution in establishing American government.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'The Constitution established the framework for federal government, created checks and balances, and protected individual rights through the Bill of Rights.', 11),

-- GED-SOCIAL Lesson 2: Civics and Government (600002)
(600002, 'The President serves a term of:', 'multiple_choice', '["2 years","4 years","6 years","8 years"]', '4 years', 1),
(600002, 'Congress consists of:', 'multiple_choice', '["Senate only","House only","Senate and House of Representatives","Supreme Court"]', 'Senate and House of Representatives', 2),
(600002, 'The Supreme Court has how many justices:', 'multiple_choice', '["7","9","11","12"]', '9', 3),
(600002, 'A bill becomes a law when:', 'multiple_choice', '["Congress passes it","The President signs it","Both Congress passes and President signs","The Supreme Court approves"]', 'Both Congress passes and President signs', 4),
(600002, 'The First Amendment protects:', 'multiple_choice', '["Right to bear arms","Freedom of speech, religion, press","Right to a fair trial","Protection from searches"]', 'Freedom of speech, religion, press', 5),
(600002, 'Federalism divides power between:', 'multiple_choice', '["President and Congress","National and state governments","Courts and legislature","Military and civilians"]', 'National and state governments', 6),
(600002, 'Checks and balances prevent:', 'multiple_choice', '["Elections","Any branch from becoming too powerful","State governments","Individual rights"]', 'Any branch from becoming too powerful', 7),
(600002, 'Citizens can participate in democracy by:', 'multiple_choice', '["Only voting","Voting, contacting representatives, jury duty","Paying taxes only","Nothing"]', 'Voting, contacting representatives, jury duty', 8),
(600002, 'The electoral college:', 'multiple_choice', '["Elects Congress","Elects the President","Appoints judges","Passes laws"]', 'Elects the President', 9),
(600002, 'Due process means:', 'multiple_choice', '["Quick trials","Fair legal procedures","No trials","Automatic conviction"]', 'Fair legal procedures', 10),
(600002, 'Explain how checks and balances work in the US government.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Each branch can limit the others: Congress passes laws, President can veto, Supreme Court can declare laws unconstitutional.', 11),

-- GED-SOCIAL Lesson 3: Economics Fundamentals (600003)
(600003, 'Supply and demand determine:', 'multiple_choice', '["Government policy","Prices in a market economy","Weather patterns","Population growth"]', 'Prices in a market economy', 1),
(600003, 'When demand increases and supply stays the same, prices:', 'multiple_choice', '["Decrease","Increase","Stay the same","Disappear"]', 'Increase', 2),
(600003, 'GDP stands for:', 'multiple_choice', '["General Domestic Product","Gross Domestic Product","Government Domestic Policy","Great Domestic Program"]', 'Gross Domestic Product', 3),
(600003, 'Inflation is:', 'multiple_choice', '["Decreasing prices","Rising prices over time","Stable prices","No prices"]', 'Rising prices over time', 4),
(600003, 'The Federal Reserve controls:', 'multiple_choice', '["Foreign policy","Monetary policy and interest rates","Immigration","Education"]', 'Monetary policy and interest rates', 5),
(600003, 'A recession is:', 'multiple_choice', '["Economic growth","Economic decline for two or more quarters","Inflation","Deflation"]', 'Economic decline for two or more quarters', 6),
(600003, 'Unemployment rate measures:', 'multiple_choice', '["Total population","People looking for work who cannot find it","All workers","Retired people"]', 'People looking for work who cannot find it', 7),
(600003, 'Fiscal policy involves:', 'multiple_choice', '["Interest rates","Government spending and taxation","Trade agreements","Banking regulations"]', 'Government spending and taxation', 8),
(600003, 'A market economy is based on:', 'multiple_choice', '["Government control","Private ownership and free markets","Central planning","No trade"]', 'Private ownership and free markets', 9),
(600003, 'Opportunity cost is:', 'multiple_choice', '["The price of goods","What you give up when making a choice","Government taxes","Business profits"]', 'What you give up when making a choice', 10),
(600003, 'Explain how supply and demand affect prices in a market economy.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'When demand exceeds supply, prices rise. When supply exceeds demand, prices fall. Equilibrium occurs when supply equals demand.', 11),

-- GED-SOCIAL Lesson 4: Geography Essentials (600004)
(600004, 'Latitude lines run:', 'multiple_choice', '["North to South","East to West","Diagonally","Randomly"]', 'East to West', 1),
(600004, 'Longitude lines run:', 'multiple_choice', '["East to West","North to South","Horizontally","Curved"]', 'North to South', 2),
(600004, 'The equator is at:', 'multiple_choice', '["0 degrees latitude","0 degrees longitude","90 degrees latitude","180 degrees longitude"]', '0 degrees latitude', 3),
(600004, 'Climate is different from weather because:', 'multiple_choice', '["Climate changes daily","Climate is long-term average conditions","They are the same","Weather is long-term"]', 'Climate is long-term average conditions', 4),
(600004, 'Natural resources include:', 'multiple_choice', '["Only oil","Water, minerals, forests, and land","Only manufactured goods","Only renewable resources"]', 'Water, minerals, forests, and land', 5),
(600004, 'Urbanization is:', 'multiple_choice', '["Movement to rural areas","Growth of cities and urban populations","Decline of cities","Agricultural expansion"]', 'Growth of cities and urban populations', 6),
(600004, 'Human geography studies:', 'multiple_choice', '["Only physical features","How humans interact with their environment","Only mountains","Only rivers"]', 'How humans interact with their environment', 7),
(600004, 'A region is defined by:', 'multiple_choice', '["Random boundaries","Common characteristics","Only political borders","Size only"]', 'Common characteristics', 8),
(600004, 'Globalization has led to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Less trade","Increased interconnection between countries","Isolation","No change"]', 'Increased interconnection between countries', 9),
(600004, 'Maps use scale to show:', 'multiple_choice', '["Colors","The relationship between map distance and real distance","Only borders","Only cities"]', 'The relationship between map distance and real distance', 10),
(600004, 'Explain how geography influences human settlement patterns.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'People settle near water sources, fertile land, and favorable climates. Geographic barriers like mountains affect trade and cultural development.', 11),

-- GED-SOCIAL Lesson 5: World History Overview (600005)
(600005, 'Ancient civilizations developed near:', 'multiple_choice', '["Deserts only","Rivers and water sources","Mountains only","Forests only"]', 'Rivers and water sources', 1),
(600005, 'The Renaissance began in:', 'multiple_choice', '["England","Italy","France","Germany"]', 'Italy', 2),
(600005, 'The Industrial Revolution started in:', 'multiple_choice', '["United States","Great Britain","France","Germany"]', 'Great Britain', 3),
(600005, 'World War II ended in:', 'multiple_choice', '["1943","1944","1945","1946"]', '1945', 4),
(600005, 'The Cold War was between:', 'multiple_choice', '["US and Britain","US and Soviet Union","Britain and France","China and Japan"]', 'US and Soviet Union', 5),
(600005, 'Colonialism involved:', 'multiple_choice', '["Trade only","European powers controlling other territories","Peaceful cooperation","No expansion"]', 'European powers controlling other territories', 6),
(600005, 'The United Nations was formed to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Start wars","Promote international peace and cooperation","Control trade","Govern countries"]', 'Promote international peace and cooperation', 7),
(600005, 'Nationalism is:', 'multiple_choice', '["Loyalty to all countries","Strong identification with one nation","Internationalism","Isolationism"]', 'Strong identification with one nation', 8),
(600005, 'The fall of the Berlin Wall symbolized:', 'multiple_choice', '["Start of Cold War","End of Cold War division","World War II","Industrial Revolution"]', 'End of Cold War division', 9),
(600005, 'Imperialism involved:', 'multiple_choice', '["Peaceful trade","Extending power over other nations","Democratic movements","Isolationism"]', 'Extending power over other nations', 10),
(600005, 'Describe major causes and effects of World War II.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Causes included fascism, territorial expansion, and Treaty of Versailles resentment. Effects included millions of deaths, the Holocaust, and the Cold War.', 11),

-- GED-SOCIAL Lesson 6: Reading Social Studies Documents (600006)
(600006, 'Primary sources are:', 'multiple_choice', '["Textbooks","Original documents from the time period","Encyclopedia articles","Summaries"]', 'Original documents from the time period', 1),
(600006, 'Secondary sources:', 'multiple_choice', '["Are original documents","Analyze or interpret primary sources","Are always unreliable","Are only photographs"]', 'Analyze or interpret primary sources', 2),
(600006, 'When reading historical documents, consider:', 'multiple_choice', '["Only the words","Context, author, and purpose","Nothing","Only the date"]', 'Context, author, and purpose', 3),
(600006, 'Bias in sources means:', 'multiple_choice', '["Complete accuracy","A particular viewpoint or prejudice","No opinion","Scientific fact"]', 'A particular viewpoint or prejudice', 4),
(600006, 'Political cartoons use:', 'multiple_choice', '["Only words","Symbols and exaggeration to make points","Only facts","No imagery"]', 'Symbols and exaggeration to make points', 5),
(600006, 'Comparing multiple sources helps:', 'multiple_choice', '["Confuse readers","Get a more complete picture","Nothing","Waste time"]', 'Get a more complete picture', 6),
(600006, 'Charts and graphs in social studies show:', 'multiple_choice', '["Random information","Data and trends","Only opinions","Nothing useful"]', 'Data and trends', 7),
(600006, 'The main idea of a passage is:', 'multiple_choice', '["A minor detail","The central point the author makes","The first sentence only","The last sentence only"]', 'The central point the author makes', 8),
(600006, 'Supporting details:', 'multiple_choice', '["Contradict the main idea","Provide evidence for the main idea","Are unrelated","Are always statistics"]', 'Provide evidence for the main idea', 9),
(600006, 'Drawing conclusions requires:', 'multiple_choice', '["Guessing","Using evidence to make logical inferences","Ignoring facts","Only opinions"]', 'Using evidence to make logical inferences', 10),
(600006, 'Explain how to identify bias in historical documents.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Look for loaded language, one-sided arguments, missing perspectives, and consider the authors background and purpose.', 11),

-- GED-SOCIAL Lesson 7: Data Analysis in Social Studies (600007)
(600007, 'A bar graph is best for:', 'multiple_choice', '["Showing trends over time","Comparing different categories","Showing parts of a whole","Displaying locations"]', 'Comparing different categories', 1),
(600007, 'A line graph shows:', 'multiple_choice', '["Categories","Changes over time","Parts of a whole","Geographic data"]', 'Changes over time', 2),
(600007, 'A pie chart displays:', 'multiple_choice', '["Trends","Parts of a whole as percentages","Comparisons over time","Geographic features"]', 'Parts of a whole as percentages', 3),
(600007, 'When reading a table, first look at:', 'multiple_choice', '["Random cells","Title and column headers","Only numbers","Nothing"]', 'Title and column headers', 4),
(600007, 'Population pyramids show:', 'multiple_choice', '["Economic data","Age and gender distribution","Political parties","Geographic features"]', 'Age and gender distribution', 5),
(600007, 'A map legend explains:', 'multiple_choice', '["Nothing","Symbols and colors used on the map","Only borders","Only cities"]', 'Symbols and colors used on the map', 6),
(600007, 'Correlation means:', 'multiple_choice', '["Causation","A relationship between two variables","No connection","Random chance"]', 'A relationship between two variables', 7),
(600007, 'Correlation does not always mean:', 'multiple_choice', '["Relationship","Causation","Data","Variables"]', 'Causation', 8),
(600007, 'Trends in data show:', 'multiple_choice', '["Random patterns","General direction or pattern over time","No information","Only past events"]', 'General direction or pattern over time', 9),
(600007, 'Interpreting data requires:', 'multiple_choice', '["Ignoring context","Understanding what the data represents","Guessing","No analysis"]', 'Understanding what the data represents', 10),
(600007, 'Describe how to interpret trends in a line graph.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Identify the variables on each axis, note the direction of the line (increasing, decreasing, stable), and consider what factors might cause the trend.', 11),

-- GED-SOCIAL Lesson 8: Writing Extended Responses (600008)
(600008, 'An effective thesis statement:', 'multiple_choice', '["Is vague","Makes a clear argument","Asks a question","Lists facts only"]', 'Makes a clear argument', 1),
(600008, 'Evidence in social studies writing comes from:', 'multiple_choice', '["Personal opinion only","Documents, data, and sources","Imagination","Guessing"]', 'Documents, data, and sources', 2),
(600008, 'A well-organized essay has:', 'multiple_choice', '["No structure","Introduction, body paragraphs, conclusion","Only one paragraph","Random order"]', 'Introduction, body paragraphs, conclusion', 3),
(600008, 'Topic sentences:', 'multiple_choice', '["End paragraphs","Introduce the main idea of each paragraph","Are unnecessary","Only appear in conclusions"]', 'Introduce the main idea of each paragraph', 4),
(600008, 'Transitions help:', 'multiple_choice', '["Confuse readers","Connect ideas smoothly","End essays","Start essays only"]', 'Connect ideas smoothly', 5),
(600008, 'When citing evidence, you should:', 'multiple_choice', '["Make it up","Quote or paraphrase accurately","Ignore sources","Only use opinions"]', 'Quote or paraphrase accurately', 6),
(600008, 'Analysis means:', 'multiple_choice', '["Summarizing only","Explaining how evidence supports your argument","Copying text","Listing facts"]', 'Explaining how evidence supports your argument', 7),
(600008, 'A conclusion should:', 'multiple_choice', '["Introduce new ideas","Summarize main points and restate thesis","Be very long","Ignore the thesis"]', 'Summarize main points and restate thesis', 8),
(600008, 'Counterarguments:', 'multiple_choice', '["Weaken your essay","Show you understand opposing views","Are unnecessary","Should be ignored"]', 'Show you understand opposing views', 9),
(600008, 'Proofreading checks for:', 'multiple_choice', '["Nothing","Grammar, spelling, and clarity errors","Only length","Only formatting"]', 'Grammar, spelling, and clarity errors', 10),
(600008, 'Explain the components of an effective extended response essay.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Include a clear thesis, organized paragraphs with evidence, analysis connecting evidence to argument, and a conclusion restating the main point.', 11),

-- GED-SOCIAL Lesson 9: Critical Thinking Skills (600009)
(600009, 'Cause and effect identifies:', 'multiple_choice', '["Random events","Why something happened and its results","Only dates","Only people"]', 'Why something happened and its results', 1),
(600009, 'Comparing and contrasting examines:', 'multiple_choice', '["Only similarities","Similarities and differences","Only differences","Nothing"]', 'Similarities and differences', 2),
(600009, 'Making inferences means:', 'multiple_choice', '["Guessing randomly","Drawing conclusions from evidence","Ignoring facts","Copying text"]', 'Drawing conclusions from evidence', 3),
(600009, 'Evaluating sources involves:', 'multiple_choice', '["Accepting everything","Assessing reliability and credibility","Ignoring sources","Using only one source"]', 'Assessing reliability and credibility', 4),
(600009, 'Point of view affects:', 'multiple_choice', '["Nothing","How information is presented","Only fiction","Only science"]', 'How information is presented', 5),
(600009, 'Fact vs. opinion: facts are:', 'multiple_choice', '["Personal beliefs","Verifiable statements","Always true","Never in documents"]', 'Verifiable statements', 6),
(600009, 'Synthesizing information means:', 'multiple_choice', '["Copying","Combining information from multiple sources","Using one source","Ignoring sources"]', 'Combining information from multiple sources', 7),
(600009, 'Historical context helps understand:', 'multiple_choice', '["Nothing","Events within their time period","Only modern events","Only ancient events"]', 'Events within their time period', 8),
(600009, 'Analyzing arguments involves:', 'multiple_choice', '["Accepting all claims","Examining evidence and reasoning","Ignoring evidence","Only agreeing"]', 'Examining evidence and reasoning', 9),
(600009, 'Critical thinking requires:', 'multiple_choice', '["Accepting everything","Questioning and evaluating information","No effort","Memorization only"]', 'Questioning and evaluating information', 10),
(600009, 'Explain how to distinguish between fact and opinion in social studies texts.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Facts can be verified with evidence and are objective. Opinions express beliefs or judgments and use words like should, best, or believe.', 11),

-- GED-SOCIAL Lesson 10: Test-Taking Strategies (600010)
(600010, 'Before reading passages, you should:', 'multiple_choice', '["Skip to questions","Preview questions to know what to look for","Ignore questions","Read randomly"]', 'Preview questions to know what to look for', 1),
(600010, 'Time management means:', 'multiple_choice', '["Spending all time on one question","Pacing yourself through all questions","Rushing","Ignoring time"]', 'Pacing yourself through all questions', 2),
(600010, 'Process of elimination:', 'multiple_choice', '["Adds confusion","Helps narrow down answer choices","Wastes time","Should be avoided"]', 'Helps narrow down answer choices', 3),
(600010, 'When stuck on a question:', 'multiple_choice', '["Give up","Skip it and return later","Spend unlimited time","Leave it blank"]', 'Skip it and return later', 4),
(600010, 'Key words in questions help:', 'multiple_choice', '["Confuse you","Identify what is being asked","Nothing","Waste time"]', 'Identify what is being asked', 5),
(600010, 'For extended response questions:', 'multiple_choice', '["Write without planning","Plan your response before writing","Skip them","Write one sentence"]', 'Plan your response before writing', 6),
(600010, 'Reading all answer choices:', 'multiple_choice', '["Wastes time","Ensures you pick the best answer","Is unnecessary","Should be avoided"]', 'Ensures you pick the best answer', 7),
(600010, 'Educated guessing is:', 'multiple_choice', '["Random","Using knowledge to eliminate wrong answers","Cheating","Always wrong"]', 'Using knowledge to eliminate wrong answers', 8),
(600010, 'Reviewing your answers:', 'multiple_choice', '["Wastes time","Helps catch errors","Changes nothing","Should be avoided"]', 'Helps catch errors', 9),
(600010, 'Staying calm during the test:', 'multiple_choice', '["Is impossible","Helps you think clearly","Wastes time","Is unnecessary"]', 'Helps you think clearly', 10),
(600010, 'Describe effective strategies for the GED Social Studies test.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Strategies include previewing questions, managing time, using process of elimination, planning extended responses, and reviewing answers.', 11);
command:
mysql --batch --raw --column-names --default-character-set=utf8mb4 --host gateway02.us-east-1.prod.aws.tidbcloud.com --port 4000 --user 2KiCrCqNDFcXt7P.577d1a63eeb5 --database P2Dpw9MQsmxzdUQCfGjV6b --execute INSERT INTO quiz_questions (lessonId, question, questionType, options, correctAnswer, questionOrder) VALUES
-- GED-SOCIAL Lesson 1: US History Foundations (600001)
(600001, 'The Declaration of Independence was signed in:', 'multiple_choice', '["1775","1776","1777","1787"]', '1776', 1),
(600001, 'The Constitution replaced the:', 'multiple_choice', '["Declaration of Independence","Articles of Confederation","Bill of Rights","Magna Carta"]', 'Articles of Confederation', 2),
(600001, 'The Bill of Rights consists of:', 'multiple_choice', '["The first 5 amendments","The first 10 amendments","The first 15 amendments","The first 20 amendments"]', 'The first 10 amendments', 3),
(600001, 'The three branches of government are:', 'multiple_choice', '["Federal, State, Local","Executive, Legislative, Judicial","President, Congress, Courts","Senate, House, Cabinet"]', 'Executive, Legislative, Judicial', 4),
(600001, 'The Civil War was primarily fought over:', 'multiple_choice', '["Taxation","Slavery and states rights","Foreign policy","Trade agreements"]', 'Slavery and states rights', 5),
(600001, 'The Emancipation Proclamation freed:', 'multiple_choice', '["All slaves","Slaves in Confederate states","Northern slaves","No one"]', 'Slaves in Confederate states', 6),
(600001, 'The Industrial Revolution brought:', 'multiple_choice', '["Agricultural decline","Factory-based manufacturing","Less urbanization","Fewer inventions"]', 'Factory-based manufacturing', 7),
(600001, 'World War I began in:', 'multiple_choice', '["1912","1914","1916","1918"]', '1914', 8),
(600001, 'The Great Depression started with:', 'multiple_choice', '["World War I","Stock market crash of 1929","World War II","The New Deal"]', 'Stock market crash of 1929', 9),
(600001, 'The New Deal was created by President:', 'multiple_choice', '["Herbert Hoover","Franklin D. Roosevelt","Harry Truman","Dwight Eisenhower"]', 'Franklin D. Roosevelt', 10),
(600001, 'Explain the significance of the Constitution in establishing American government.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'The Constitution established the framework for federal government, created checks and balances, and protected individual rights through the Bill of Rights.', 11),

-- GED-SOCIAL Lesson 2: Civics and Government (600002)
(600002, 'The President serves a term of:', 'multiple_choice', '["2 years","4 years","6 years","8 years"]', '4 years', 1),
(600002, 'Congress consists of:', 'multiple_choice', '["Senate only","House only","Senate and House of Representatives","Supreme Court"]', 'Senate and House of Representatives', 2),
(600002, 'The Supreme Court has how many justices:', 'multiple_choice', '["7","9","11","12"]', '9', 3),
(600002, 'A bill becomes a law when:', 'multiple_choice', '["Congress passes it","The President signs it","Both Congress passes and President signs","The Supreme Court approves"]', 'Both Congress passes and President signs', 4),
(600002, 'The First Amendment protects:', 'multiple_choice', '["Right to bear arms","Freedom of speech, religion, press","Right to a fair trial","Protection from searches"]', 'Freedom of speech, religion, press', 5),
(600002, 'Federalism divides power between:', 'multiple_choice', '["President and Congress","National and state governments","Courts and legislature","Military and civilians"]', 'National and state governments', 6),
(600002, 'Checks and balances prevent:', 'multiple_choice', '["Elections","Any branch from becoming too powerful","State governments","Individual rights"]', 'Any branch from becoming too powerful', 7),
(600002, 'Citizens can participate in democracy by:', 'multiple_choice', '["Only voting","Voting, contacting representatives, jury duty","Paying taxes only","Nothing"]', 'Voting, contacting representatives, jury duty', 8),
(600002, 'The electoral college:', 'multiple_choice', '["Elects Congress","Elects the President","Appoints judges","Passes laws"]', 'Elects the President', 9),
(600002, 'Due process means:', 'multiple_choice', '["Quick trials","Fair legal procedures","No trials","Automatic conviction"]', 'Fair legal procedures', 10),
(600002, 'Explain how checks and balances work in the US government.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Each branch can limit the others: Congress passes laws, President can veto, Supreme Court can declare laws unconstitutional.', 11),

-- GED-SOCIAL Lesson 3: Economics Fundamentals (600003)
(600003, 'Supply and demand determine:', 'multiple_choice', '["Government policy","Prices in a market economy","Weather patterns","Population growth"]', 'Prices in a market economy', 1),
(600003, 'When demand increases and supply stays the same, prices:', 'multiple_choice', '["Decrease","Increase","Stay the same","Disappear"]', 'Increase', 2),
(600003, 'GDP stands for:', 'multiple_choice', '["General Domestic Product","Gross Domestic Product","Government Domestic Policy","Great Domestic Program"]', 'Gross Domestic Product', 3),
(600003, 'Inflation is:', 'multiple_choice', '["Decreasing prices","Rising prices over time","Stable prices","No prices"]', 'Rising prices over time', 4),
(600003, 'The Federal Reserve controls:', 'multiple_choice', '["Foreign policy","Monetary policy and interest rates","Immigration","Education"]', 'Monetary policy and interest rates', 5),
(600003, 'A recession is:', 'multiple_choice', '["Economic growth","Economic decline for two or more quarters","Inflation","Deflation"]', 'Economic decline for two or more quarters', 6),
(600003, 'Unemployment rate measures:', 'multiple_choice', '["Total population","People looking for work who cannot find it","All workers","Retired people"]', 'People looking for work who cannot find it', 7),
(600003, 'Fiscal policy involves:', 'multiple_choice', '["Interest rates","Government spending and taxation","Trade agreements","Banking regulations"]', 'Government spending and taxation', 8),
(600003, 'A market economy is based on:', 'multiple_choice', '["Government control","Private ownership and free markets","Central planning","No trade"]', 'Private ownership and free markets', 9),
(600003, 'Opportunity cost is:', 'multiple_choice', '["The price of goods","What you give up when making a choice","Government taxes","Business profits"]', 'What you give up when making a choice', 10),
(600003, 'Explain how supply and demand affect prices in a market economy.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'When demand exceeds supply, prices rise. When supply exceeds demand, prices fall. Equilibrium occurs when supply equals demand.', 11),

-- GED-SOCIAL Lesson 4: Geography Essentials (600004)
(600004, 'Latitude lines run:', 'multiple_choice', '["North to South","East to West","Diagonally","Randomly"]', 'East to West', 1),
(600004, 'Longitude lines run:', 'multiple_choice', '["East to West","North to South","Horizontally","Curved"]', 'North to South', 2),
(600004, 'The equator is at:', 'multiple_choice', '["0 degrees latitude","0 degrees longitude","90 degrees latitude","180 degrees longitude"]', '0 degrees latitude', 3),
(600004, 'Climate is different from weather because:', 'multiple_choice', '["Climate changes daily","Climate is long-term average conditions","They are the same","Weather is long-term"]', 'Climate is long-term average conditions', 4),
(600004, 'Natural resources include:', 'multiple_choice', '["Only oil","Water, minerals, forests, and land","Only manufactured goods","Only renewable resources"]', 'Water, minerals, forests, and land', 5),
(600004, 'Urbanization is:', 'multiple_choice', '["Movement to rural areas","Growth of cities and urban populations","Decline of cities","Agricultural expansion"]', 'Growth of cities and urban populations', 6),
(600004, 'Human geography studies:', 'multiple_choice', '["Only physical features","How humans interact with their environment","Only mountains","Only rivers"]', 'How humans interact with their environment', 7),
(600004, 'A region is defined by:', 'multiple_choice', '["Random boundaries","Common characteristics","Only political borders","Size only"]', 'Common characteristics', 8),
(600004, 'Globalization has led to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Less trade","Increased interconnection between countries","Isolation","No change"]', 'Increased interconnection between countries', 9),
(600004, 'Maps use scale to show:', 'multiple_choice', '["Colors","The relationship between map distance and real distance","Only borders","Only cities"]', 'The relationship between map distance and real distance', 10),
(600004, 'Explain how geography influences human settlement patterns.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'People settle near water sources, fertile land, and favorable climates. Geographic barriers like mountains affect trade and cultural development.', 11),

-- GED-SOCIAL Lesson 5: World History Overview (600005)
(600005, 'Ancient civilizations developed near:', 'multiple_choice', '["Deserts only","Rivers and water sources","Mountains only","Forests only"]', 'Rivers and water sources', 1),
(600005, 'The Renaissance began in:', 'multiple_choice', '["England","Italy","France","Germany"]', 'Italy', 2),
(600005, 'The Industrial Revolution started in:', 'multiple_choice', '["United States","Great Britain","France","Germany"]', 'Great Britain', 3),
(600005, 'World War II ended in:', 'multiple_choice', '["1943","1944","1945","1946"]', '1945', 4),
(600005, 'The Cold War was between:', 'multiple_choice', '["US and Britain","US and Soviet Union","Britain and France","China and Japan"]', 'US and Soviet Union', 5),
(600005, 'Colonialism involved:', 'multiple_choice', '["Trade only","European powers controlling other territories","Peaceful cooperation","No expansion"]', 'European powers controlling other territories', 6),
(600005, 'The United Nations was formed to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Start wars","Promote international peace and cooperation","Control trade","Govern countries"]', 'Promote international peace and cooperation', 7),
(600005, 'Nationalism is:', 'multiple_choice', '["Loyalty to all countries","Strong identification with one nation","Internationalism","Isolationism"]', 'Strong identification with one nation', 8),
(600005, 'The fall of the Berlin Wall symbolized:', 'multiple_choice', '["Start of Cold War","End of Cold War division","World War II","Industrial Revolution"]', 'End of Cold War division', 9),
(600005, 'Imperialism involved:', 'multiple_choice', '["Peaceful trade","Extending power over other nations","Democratic movements","Isolationism"]', 'Extending power over other nations', 10),
(600005, 'Describe major causes and effects of World War II.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Causes included fascism, territorial expansion, and Treaty of Versailles resentment. Effects included millions of deaths, the Holocaust, and the Cold War.', 11),

-- GED-SOCIAL Lesson 6: Reading Social Studies Documents (600006)
(600006, 'Primary sources are:', 'multiple_choice', '["Textbooks","Original documents from the time period","Encyclopedia articles","Summaries"]', 'Original documents from the time period', 1),
(600006, 'Secondary sources:', 'multiple_choice', '["Are original documents","Analyze or interpret primary sources","Are always unreliable","Are only photographs"]', 'Analyze or interpret primary sources', 2),
(600006, 'When reading historical documents, consider:', 'multiple_choice', '["Only the words","Context, author, and purpose","Nothing","Only the date"]', 'Context, author, and purpose', 3),
(600006, 'Bias in sources means:', 'multiple_choice', '["Complete accuracy","A particular viewpoint or prejudice","No opinion","Scientific fact"]', 'A particular viewpoint or prejudice', 4),
(600006, 'Political cartoons use:', 'multiple_choice', '["Only words","Symbols and exaggeration to make points","Only facts","No imagery"]', 'Symbols and exaggeration to make points', 5),
(600006, 'Comparing multiple sources helps:', 'multiple_choice', '["Confuse readers","Get a more complete picture","Nothing","Waste time"]', 'Get a more complete picture', 6),
(600006, 'Charts and graphs in social studies show:', 'multiple_choice', '["Random information","Data and trends","Only opinions","Nothing useful"]', 'Data and trends', 7),
(600006, 'The main idea of a passage is:', 'multiple_choice', '["A minor detail","The central point the author makes","The first sentence only","The last sentence only"]', 'The central point the author makes', 8),
(600006, 'Supporting details:', 'multiple_choice', '["Contradict the main idea","Provide evidence for the main idea","Are unrelated","Are always statistics"]', 'Provide evidence for the main idea', 9),
(600006, 'Drawing conclusions requires:', 'multiple_choice', '["Guessing","Using evidence to make logical inferences","Ignoring facts","Only opinions"]', 'Using evidence to make logical inferences', 10),
(600006, 'Explain how to identify bias in historical documents.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Look for loaded language, one-sided arguments, missing perspectives, and consider the authors background and purpose.', 11),

-- GED-SOCIAL Lesson 7: Data Analysis in Social Studies (600007)
(600007, 'A bar graph is best for:', 'multiple_choice', '["Showing trends over time","Comparing different categories","Showing parts of a whole","Displaying locations"]', 'Comparing different categories', 1),
(600007, 'A line graph shows:', 'multiple_choice', '["Categories","Changes over time","Parts of a whole","Geographic data"]', 'Changes over time', 2),
(600007, 'A pie chart displays:', 'multiple_choice', '["Trends","Parts of a whole as percentages","Comparisons over time","Geographic features"]', 'Parts of a whole as percentages', 3),
(600007, 'When reading a table, first look at:', 'multiple_choice', '["Random cells","Title and column headers","Only numbers","Nothing"]', 'Title and column headers', 4),
(600007, 'Population pyramids show:', 'multiple_choice', '["Economic data","Age and gender distribution","Political parties","Geographic features"]', 'Age and gender distribution', 5),
(600007, 'A map legend explains:', 'multiple_choice', '["Nothing","Symbols and colors used on the map","Only borders","Only cities"]', 'Symbols and colors used on the map', 6),
(600007, 'Correlation means:', 'multiple_choice', '["Causation","A relationship between two variables","No connection","Random chance"]', 'A relationship between two variables', 7),
(600007, 'Correlation does not always mean:', 'multiple_choice', '["Relationship","Causation","Data","Variables"]', 'Causation', 8),
(600007, 'Trends in data show:', 'multiple_choice', '["Random patterns","General direction or pattern over time","No information","Only past events"]', 'General direction or pattern over time', 9),
(600007, 'Interpreting data requires:', 'multiple_choice', '["Ignoring context","Understanding what the data represents","Guessing","No analysis"]', 'Understanding what the data represents', 10),
(600007, 'Describe how to interpret trends in a line graph.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Identify the variables on each axis, note the direction of the line (increasing, decreasing, stable), and consider what factors might cause the trend.', 11),

-- GED-SOCIAL Lesson 8: Writing Extended Responses (600008)
(600008, 'An effective thesis statement:', 'multiple_choice', '["Is vague","Makes a clear argument","Asks a question","Lists facts only"]', 'Makes a clear argument', 1),
(600008, 'Evidence in social studies writing comes from:', 'multiple_choice', '["Personal opinion only","Documents, data, and sources","Imagination","Guessing"]', 'Documents, data, and sources', 2),
(600008, 'A well-organized essay has:', 'multiple_choice', '["No structure","Introduction, body paragraphs, conclusion","Only one paragraph","Random order"]', 'Introduction, body paragraphs, conclusion', 3),
(600008, 'Topic sentences:', 'multiple_choice', '["End paragraphs","Introduce the main idea of each paragraph","Are unnecessary","Only appear in conclusions"]', 'Introduce the main idea of each paragraph', 4),
(600008, 'Transitions help:', 'multiple_choice', '["Confuse readers","Connect ideas smoothly","End essays","Start essays only"]', 'Connect ideas smoothly', 5),
(600008, 'When citing evidence, you should:', 'multiple_choice', '["Make it up","Quote or paraphrase accurately","Ignore sources","Only use opinions"]', 'Quote or paraphrase accurately', 6),
(600008, 'Analysis means:', 'multiple_choice', '["Summarizing only","Explaining how evidence supports your argument","Copying text","Listing facts"]', 'Explaining how evidence supports your argument', 7),
(600008, 'A conclusion should:', 'multiple_choice', '["Introduce new ideas","Summarize main points and restate thesis","Be very long","Ignore the thesis"]', 'Summarize main points and restate thesis', 8),
(600008, 'Counterarguments:', 'multiple_choice', '["Weaken your essay","Show you understand opposing views","Are unnecessary","Should be ignored"]', 'Show you understand opposing views', 9),
(600008, 'Proofreading checks for:', 'multiple_choice', '["Nothing","Grammar, spelling, and clarity errors","Only length","Only formatting"]', 'Grammar, spelling, and clarity errors', 10),
(600008, 'Explain the components of an effective extended response essay.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Include a clear thesis, organized paragraphs with evidence, analysis connecting evidence to argument, and a conclusion restating the main point.', 11),

-- GED-SOCIAL Lesson 9: Critical Thinking Skills (600009)
(600009, 'Cause and effect identifies:', 'multiple_choice', '["Random events","Why something happened and its results","Only dates","Only people"]', 'Why something happened and its results', 1),
(600009, 'Comparing and contrasting examines:', 'multiple_choice', '["Only similarities","Similarities and differences","Only differences","Nothing"]', 'Similarities and differences', 2),
(600009, 'Making inferences means:', 'multiple_choice', '["Guessing randomly","Drawing conclusions from evidence","Ignoring facts","Copying text"]', 'Drawing conclusions from evidence', 3),
(600009, 'Evaluating sources involves:', 'multiple_choice', '["Accepting everything","Assessing reliability and credibility","Ignoring sources","Using only one source"]', 'Assessing reliability and credibility', 4),
(600009, 'Point of view affects:', 'multiple_choice', '["Nothing","How information is presented","Only fiction","Only science"]', 'How information is presented', 5),
(600009, 'Fact vs. opinion: facts are:', 'multiple_choice', '["Personal beliefs","Verifiable statements","Always true","Never in documents"]', 'Verifiable statements', 6),
(600009, 'Synthesizing information means:', 'multiple_choice', '["Copying","Combining information from multiple sources","Using one source","Ignoring sources"]', 'Combining information from multiple sources', 7),
(600009, 'Historical context helps understand:', 'multiple_choice', '["Nothing","Events within their time period","Only modern events","Only ancient events"]', 'Events within their time period', 8),
(600009, 'Analyzing arguments involves:', 'multiple_choice', '["Accepting all claims","Examining evidence and reasoning","Ignoring evidence","Only agreeing"]', 'Examining evidence and reasoning', 9),
(600009, 'Critical thinking requires:', 'multiple_choice', '["Accepting everything","Questioning and evaluating information","No effort","Memorization only"]', 'Questioning and evaluating information', 10),
(600009, 'Explain how to distinguish between fact and opinion in social studies texts.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Facts can be verified with evidence and are objective. Opinions express beliefs or judgments and use words like should, best, or believe.', 11),

-- GED-SOCIAL Lesson 10: Test-Taking Strategies (600010)
(600010, 'Before reading passages, you should:', 'multiple_choice', '["Skip to questions","Preview questions to know what to look for","Ignore questions","Read randomly"]', 'Preview questions to know what to look for', 1),
(600010, 'Time management means:', 'multiple_choice', '["Spending all time on one question","Pacing yourself through all questions","Rushing","Ignoring time"]', 'Pacing yourself through all questions', 2),
(600010, 'Process of elimination:', 'multiple_choice', '["Adds confusion","Helps narrow down answer choices","Wastes time","Should be avoided"]', 'Helps narrow down answer choices', 3),
(600010, 'When stuck on a question:', 'multiple_choice', '["Give up","Skip it and return later","Spend unlimited time","Leave it blank"]', 'Skip it and return later', 4),
(600010, 'Key words in questions help:', 'multiple_choice', '["Confuse you","Identify what is being asked","Nothing","Waste time"]', 'Identify what is being asked', 5),
(600010, 'For extended response questions:', 'multiple_choice', '["Write without planning","Plan your response before writing","Skip them","Write one sentence"]', 'Plan your response before writing', 6),
(600010, 'Reading all answer choices:', 'multiple_choice', '["Wastes time","Ensures you pick the best answer","Is unnecessary","Should be avoided"]', 'Ensures you pick the best answer', 7),
(600010, 'Educated guessing is:', 'multiple_choice', '["Random","Using knowledge to eliminate wrong answers","Cheating","Always wrong"]', 'Using knowledge to eliminate wrong answers', 8),
(600010, 'Reviewing your answers:', 'multiple_choice', '["Wastes time","Helps catch errors","Changes nothing","Should be avoided"]', 'Helps catch errors', 9),
(600010, 'Staying calm during the test:', 'multiple_choice', '["Is impossible","Helps you think clearly","Wastes time","Is unnecessary"]', 'Helps you think clearly', 10),
(600010, 'Describe effective strategies for the GED Social Studies test.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Strategies include previewing questions, managing time, using process of elimination, planning extended responses, and reviewing answers.', 11);
